NAICS-6 Description: All Other Miscellaneous Fabricated Metal Product Manufacturing
Role / Description: This UEI is Rolls-Royce Naval Marine’s Walpole, Massachusetts operation. For DDG-51 ships, public Rolls-Royce evidence ties Walpole to controllable-pitch-propeller hub and oil-distribution-box work and to production/service of naval propeller components; it should not be conflated with the Indianapolis sibling that supplies the generator-set gas-turbine prime movers.

NAICS-6 Description: Aircraft Engine and Engine Parts Manufacturing
Role / Description: GE’s Evendale/Cincinnati aircraft-engine entity produces LM2500 aeroderivative gas turbines used as the DDG-51 class’s main-propulsion engines. GE states that each Arleigh Burke-class destroyer is powered by four LM2500-family engines.

NAICS-6 Description: Air-Conditioning and Warm Air Heating Equipment and Commercial and Industrial Refrigeration Equipment Manufacturing
Parent Vendor Name: JOHNSON CONTROLS INTERNATIONAL PUBLIC LIMITED COMPANY
Role / Description: This legacy York International registration is the York, Pennsylvania HVAC/refrigeration operation. YORK equipment is used for shipboard heating, ventilation, air-conditioning, and refrigeration, but public evidence does not separate this UEI’s DDG-51 deliverable from the affiliated Johnson Controls Navy Systems entity.

NAICS-6 Description: Air-Conditioning and Warm Air Heating Equipment and Commercial and Industrial Refrigeration Equipment Manufacturing
Role / Description: Provides Navy HVAC/refrigeration and chilled-water air-conditioning equipment for DDG-51-type surface combatants. Johnson Controls Navy Systems sources describe Navy HVAC/R solutions and High Efficiency Super Capacity (HESC) air-conditioning plants; DDG-51 class ships use distributed chilled-water air-conditioning plants to cool ship systems and habitability spaces.

NAICS-6 Description: Speed Changer, Industrial High-Speed Drive, and Gear Manufacturing
Role / Description: Through Philadelphia Gear, supplies main reduction gears for DDG-51 Flight III propulsion. Timken public releases describe a long-term Bath Iron Works contract to provide main reduction gears for future Flight III Arleigh Burke/DDG-51 ships; other public Navy/industry reporting identifies shipsets of DDG-51 main reduction gears.

Public sources reviewed do not identify the specific DDG-51 item or service. Portuguese business directories describe SOCAIL as an industrial-piping/plumbing and building-construction contractor; because no reliable source ties a particular product or work package to the DDG subaward, the DDG-51 role cannot be confirmed.

NAICS-6 Description: Search, Detection, Navigation, Guidance, Aeronautical, and Nautical System and Instrument Manufacturing
Role / Description: Supplies Integrated Bridge and Navigation System (IBNS) steering-control hardware for DDG-51-class ships; a Navy procurement specifically identifies an IBNS aft steering unit.

NAICS-6 Description: Power, Distribution, and Specialty Transformer Manufacturing
Role / Description: The Menomonee Falls DRS Naval Power Systems entity manufactures naval electrical power-distribution hardware, including transformers, switchboards/load centers, power-conditioning equipment, and related controls. Public sources confirm the facility and product family, but do not disclose the exact DDG-51 unit covered by this UEI’s subaward.

Parent Vendor Name: L3HARRIS MARITIME POWER & ENERGY SOLUTIONS, INC.
Role / Description: This UEI is the Tulsa maritime power business formerly known as L-3 Westwood. It supplies MIL-SPEC shipboard switchboards and load centers used to distribute electrical power on DDG-51-class ships.

Ellwood National Forge’s Irvine, Pennsylvania operation produces large open-die steel forgings and forged shafting for naval and other heavy-duty applications. The specific DDG-51 forging covered by this subaward is not publicly identified, so no particular shaft, rotor, or coupling should be assigned.

NAICS-6 Description: Air-Conditioning and Warm Air Heating Equipment and Commercial and Industrial Refrigeration Equipment Manufacturing
Role / Description: Engineered Coil Company is Leonardo DRS’s Marlo Coil operation in High Ridge, Missouri. It manufactures custom Navy and marine heat-transfer equipment—evaporator and condenser coils, air coolers, and other HVAC/refrigeration coils—for shipboard environmental-control systems; the public record does not identify the exact DDG-51 coil assembly.

NAICS-6 Description: All Other Miscellaneous Electrical Equipment and Component Manufacturing
Role / Description: Supplies DDG-51 electrical power electronics, transformers and power-distribution/protection equipment. Espey identifies DDG-51 products such as ship-service transformers, transformer step-start equipment, high-resistance grounding transformers, static-switch systems and AN/SPY-6-related cabinets/components, supported by its military power-systems and magnetics manufacturing capability.

SPD Electrical Systems in Camden, New Jersey manufactures shock-hardened naval circuit breakers, switchgear, and power-distribution equipment. Its DDG-51 role is electrical protection and distribution hardware, distinct from L3Harris’s Tulsa switchboard assembly and Anaheim transformer businesses.

NAICS-6 Description: Electrical Contractors and Other Wiring Installation Contractors
Role / Description: Provides marine fire-protection equipment and engineering. A Hiller-approved 1½-inch stop-check valve is specifically identified as a component of the Halon fire-extinguishing system on DDG-51 through DDG-58; Hiller also designs, installs, and services shipboard detection and suppression systems.

Supplies large forged marine propulsion/stern shafting for Navy ships. Erie Forge & Steel describes forged steel marine propulsion shafts for U.S. Government/Navy customers, and public contract records identify a Navy DDG-51 stern-shaft item associated with Erie Forge and Steel.

NAICS-6 Description: Power, Distribution, and Specialty Transformer Manufacturing
Role / Description: Supplies solid-state frequency/power-conversion equipment and related power-distribution magnetics. Its ACFC-288 converts 60-Hz ship-service power to 400 Hz for AEGIS equipment on DDG-51-class ships.

NAICS-6 Description: All Other Miscellaneous General Purpose Machinery Manufacturing
Role / Description: Supplies retractable mooring bitts for DDG-51 construction. The Navy identified Entwistle as the original-equipment manufacturer and sole authorized manufacturer for these long-lead deck-machinery items.

Fabricates outfitted structural units, hull modules and other DDG-51 structural components for distributed shipbuilding work supporting HII/Ingalls DDG-51 construction. Public sources describe Gulf Copper work building select outfitted structural units and Navy destroyer structural components in Texas facilities.

ADI Technologies is a procurement/technical-services company rather than a ship-component manufacturer. Navy notices tied to this UEI show it furnishing a quick-acting watertight door for USS Preble (DDG-88), so its concrete role is sourcing that closure assembly rather than manufacturing the broader parent’s interior systems.

NAICS-6 Description: Measuring, Dispensing, and Other Pumping Equipment Manufacturing
Role / Description: Supplies centrifugal pumps and OEM parts/overhaul support for DDG-51 shipboard fluid systems. A Navy solicitation identifies Buffalo DH6080D-series centrifugal pumps for the Arleigh Burke class.

US Joiner fabricates and installs naval joiner systems such as non-structural bulkheads, doors, modular berths/furnishings, and fire-rated interior barriers. These are ship-interior and habitability construction products, distinct from Trident’s HVAC and IMECO’s machinery/electrical integration work.

Subawardee Vendor Name: L3HARRIS MARITIME POWER & ENERGY SOLUTIONS, INC
NAICS-6 Description: Power, Distribution, and Specialty Transformer Manufacturing
Role / Description: The Anaheim L3Harris Maritime Power & Energy Solutions entity manufactures naval power and distribution equipment, with its registered industry focused on specialty transformers. Public sources do not isolate the exact transformer or power package supplied to the DDG-51 program under this UEI.

Supplies propulsion bearing systems for Arleigh Burke-class destroyers. AMB lists the class among vessels equipped with its systems; its naval product line includes line-shaft, stern-tube, strut, and thrust bearings.

NAICS-6 Description: Commercial and Service Industry Machinery Manufacturing
Role / Description: Through Parker Village Marine, supplies MIL-SPEC reverse-osmosis desalination and water-purification systems installed on Arleigh Burke-class destroyers. The systems produce potable water and high-purity permeate for shipboard use.

Supplies heat-recovery evaporators/fresh-water generators that desalinate seawater into potable water. DDG-51 construction subawards cite the Maxim evaporator purchase specification, although public records do not expose the exact commercial model.

Supplies the DDG-51 class slewing-arm boat-davit system and associated replacement/overhaul kits. A Navy award for USS Stockdale (DDG-106) identifies Lake Shore as the OEM for the installed slewing-arm davit, part number 56W4-S100.

NAICS-6 Description: Power Boiler and Heat Exchanger Manufacturing
Role / Description: Supplies self-cleaning disk-stack centrifugal fuel purifiers used to remove water and solids from DDG-51 fuel-transfer streams. The DDG subaward is recorded under fuel-system purchase specification 541-10B.

Subawardee Vendor Name: DOVER PUMPS & PROCESS SOLUTIONS SEGMENT INC
NAICS-6 Description: Measuring, Dispensing, and Other Pumping Equipment Manufacturing
Parent Vendor Name: DOVER PUMPS & PROCESS SOLUTIONS SEGMENT INC
Role / Description: Supplies Blackmer rotary/hand pumps and related pump hardware for DDG-51. Public Navy item data also identifies a Blackmer relief-valve kit for the class’s aqueous-film-forming-foam firefighting pump.

NAICS-6 Description: Industrial and Commercial Fan and Blower and Air Purification Equipment Manufacturing
Role / Description: Through its HAF Navy Market/Howden American Fan business, supplied DDG-51 cooling and ventilation fans, including gas-turbine-room blowers, collective-protection-system fans, and vane-axial/centrifugal fans. Fairbanks Morse Defense acquired American Fan in 2023 and now supplies the same product line on Flight III destroyers.

NAICS-6 Description: Capacitor, Resistor, Coil, Transformer, and Other Inductor Manufacturing
Role / Description: The DDG-128/129 construction subaward is labeled “TMR rebuy ES012 10451,” but the public record does not expand TMR or identify the exact equipment. EMS manufactures naval power-conversion/degaussing electronics, transformers, and power supplies; a specific product-to-subaward match could not be confirmed.
Supplies shipboard butterfly/gate valve assemblies and replacement parts for DDG-51 piping systems. Public NSN data identifies Morpac butterfly valves with the Arleigh Burke class as an end item.
Supplies STAR-200 high-pressure air compressors for DDG-51 Flight III ships, supporting shipboard compressed-air requirements.
Supplies Navy-qualified gate, globe, check/swing-check, and ball valves for DDG-51 fluid piping. Public item records identify Milwaukee swing-check valves specifically used on the class.

Performs welding/fabrication, ship-repair, and warranty work on DDG-51-class ships. Public records tie Southcoast to DDG construction warranty support and to repair work on USS Fitzgerald (DDG-62), but do not disclose a specific construction component in the listed subaward.

NAICS-6 Description: Industrial Machinery and Equipment Merchant Wholesalers
Role / Description: Supplies/distributes and repairs naval valve and actuator hardware under the J.A. Moody name. Public item data links an ESI valve assembly to the Arleigh Burke class, but the exact valve or actuator covered by this DDG construction subaward is not publicly identified.

Portland Valve manufactures Navy ship-service ball valves and other specialty valves used on destroyers, submarines, and carriers. For DDG-51 construction, this entity’s distinct contribution is valve hardware for shipboard fluid systems, although the exact valve list is not public.

NAICS-6 Description: Electrical Apparatus and Equipment, Wiring Supplies, and Related Equipment Merchant Wholesalers
Role / Description: Supplies electrical-distribution hardware, including circuit breakers for DDG-51 weapons systems and shore-power cable adapters used on the class.

NAICS-6 Description: Fluid Power Valve and Hose Fitting Manufacturing
Role / Description: Supplies engineered fluid-control valves and manifolds for DDG-51 shipboard systems; Marotta identifies itself as a major valve/manifold supplier to the class.

Aircraft Appliances and Equipment Limited manufactures military pressure switches, control panels, power panels, and related electromechanical equipment. Public Navy notices confirm those product types for this entity, but do not identify the precise DDG-51 assembly represented by the subaward.

Supplies the DDG-51 helicopter-hangar door system and replacement components. Jered’s electrically driven folding NAVTRUSS-panel doors are installed on the class and are qualified to Navy Grade A shock requirements.

The DDG subaward is under electrical/communications purchase specification 431-01C. Dynalec manufactures rugged shipboard communications, navigation, and switching equipment—especially sound-powered telephone/communications systems—but the exact unit in this subaward is not publicly disclosed.

Subawardee Vendor Name: BAE SYSTEMS JACKSONVILLE SHIP REPAIR LLC
Role / Description: Supplies Mk 45 Mod 4 5-inch/62-caliber naval gun systems for new-construction DDG-51 destroyers. BAE Systems public materials state Mk 45 gun systems are to be installed on new-construction Arleigh Burke/DDG-51 destroyers and describe the Mod 4 naval gun system.

Leslie Controls manufactures severe-service regulating and control valves, pressure-reducing valves, and fluid-control systems. Its DDG-51 role is shipboard control-valve hardware; the public record does not identify the particular valve or service supplied under this UEI.

Supplies naval motors, motor controllers, control components, heaters, overload relays, and systems-integration support. DDG-51 technical records identify Ward Leonard motor-controller equipment installed on the class; the specific construction-line item is not public.

NAICS-6 Description: Power, Distribution, and Specialty Transformer Manufacturing
Role / Description: Supplies automatic transfer-switch/power-control assemblies and electronic modules for shipboard electrical distribution. DDG subawards are documented, and Bogue ATS-4100 modules are publicly cataloged; the exact model purchased under the cited DDG line is not disclosed.
Provided fuel for DDG-125 construction, testing, and/or sea-trial support; the subcontract description explicitly identifies fuel for the ship.
Supplies deck-machinery hardware, including the emergency quick-disconnect switch and weldment handle for the DDG-51 slewing-arm davit (SLAD).
Supplies air-moisture separation and filtration systems for DDG-51 gas-turbine air intakes, removing water droplets and contaminants before air enters the engines.

Supplies Cla-Val automatic-control, pressure-reducing, and solenoid valve assemblies/components for DDG-51 fluid systems. Public item records identify Griswold/Cla-Val valve parts with the Arleigh Burke class as an end item, but the exact valve model in this subaward is not public.

Supplies marine-interior/joiner outfitting—steel/aluminum furniture, accommodation fittings, and related interior packages. A NIST shipbuilding-industry report identifies Jamestown as a marine-interiors outfitter, but public sources do not enumerate the exact items in this DDG subaward.

NAICS-6 Description: Air-Conditioning and Warm Air Heating Equipment and Commercial and Industrial Refrigeration Equipment Manufacturing
Role / Description: Supplies Model 6995 low-pressure membrane compressed-air dehydrators selected for DDG-51 new construction and Model 7600 ECOLCELL electrochemical anti-fouling chlorinators, class-standard beginning with DDG-103.

Juniper manufactures Navy watertight, ballistic, and weathertight doors, hatches, scuttles/manholes, plus marine-ventilation components. Public sources reviewed do not identify which of these products was supplied under this DDG-51 subaward.
Manufactures and repairs waterborne-propulsion shafting—propeller shafts, intermediate shafts, and stern-tube shafts—for DDG-51 ships.

NAICS-6 Description: Other Communication and Energy Wire Manufacturing
Role / Description: Supplies low-smoke, flame-retardant shipboard electrical/fiber-optic cable and cable assemblies under the RSCC/Marmon brands. A DDG-51 construction subcontract is documented, but public records do not disclose the exact cable type or quantity.

NAICS-6 Description: Measuring, Dispensing, and Other Pumping Equipment Manufacturing
Role / Description: Supplies naval pumps and pump replacement/aftermarket components from the Warren and IMO product families for DDG-51 fluid systems. CIRCOR’s Warren/Simsite page explicitly lists DDG-51 among supported applications; the exact subaward pump model is not public.

NAICS-6 Description: Heating Equipment (except Warm Air Furnaces) Manufacturing
Role / Description: Supplies fuel immersion/flange heaters and associated temperature-sensing/control hardware for DDG-51. Public NSN data identifies an INDEECO thermocouple used on the class’s fuel immersion-flange heater.

Performs abrasive blasting, surface preparation, and protective marine coating/painting on DDG construction. Public sources confirm its naval preservation/coatings capability, but the exact DDG compartment or coating system in the listed subaward is not disclosed.

NAICS-6 Description: Commercial and Service Industry Machinery Manufacturing
Role / Description: Supplies shock-resistant vacuum toilets, flush units, blackwater collection equipment, and sewage-pumping hardware for DDG-51 sanitary systems.
Supplies and installs interior deck coverings/flooring on DDG-51 ships; a DDG construction subcontract is explicitly described as “deck covering.”

NAICS-6 Description: All Other Miscellaneous General Purpose Machinery Manufacturing
Role / Description: The DDG-128 subaward is a rebuy under ES012 11250, but public records do not identify the component. Facet/Filtration Group makes fuel-filtration, water-separation, and coalescer equipment; no reliable source was found matching a particular Facet model to this DDG line, so the exact role cannot be confirmed.

NAICS-6 Description: Aircraft Engine and Engine Parts Manufacturing
Role / Description: Supplies ionization smoke detectors for DDG-51 fire/alarm systems; the Navy identifies Meggitt P/N SAS-04398-01 as the sole approved source for the class.
Performs maintenance, repair, alterations, and fabrication on DDG-51-class ships during availabilities. Public records confirm DDG-class repair work but do not specify a new-construction component.

Supplies hydrodynamic thrust and journal bearings for DDG-51 propulsion shaft lines and rotating machinery. Public item data links a Kingsbury bearing-related assembly to the Arleigh Burke class, but the exact bearing set in this construction subaward is not disclosed.

NAICS-6 Description: Instruments and Related Products Manufacturing for Measuring, Displaying, and Controlling Industrial Process Variables
Role / Description: Supplies tank liquid-level/quantity sensors, transmitters, receivers, and indicators for DDG-51 shipboard tanks and monitoring systems.

A DDG-51 subcontract is recorded for this UEI, but public sources reviewed do not identify the delivered item or work package. SteelFab is a structural-steel fabricator; without a source connecting a specific fabricated assembly to the DDG subaward, the role cannot be confirmed.

NAICS-6 Description: Overhead Traveling Crane, Hoist, and Monorail System Manufacturing
Role / Description: Supplies slewing-arm davits and deck machinery used to launch and recover small boats. Appleton is a Navy-contracted SLAD supplier; public sources reviewed do not identify the exact davit model or hull number covered by this listed DDG construction subaward.

NAICS-6 Description: Copper Rolling, Drawing, Extruding, and Alloying
Role / Description: Supplies shipboard electrical wire and cable for DDG-51 construction, including low-smoke/zero-halogen Navy cable families such as MIL-DTL-24643, MIL-DTL-24640, and MIL-DTL-915. The DDG subcontract description is simply “Cable,” so the exact specification and circuit are not public.
Supplies marine ball valves and inline/twinline check valves for DDG-51 piping systems; BNL’s own catalog lists DDG-51 among supported Navy ship classes.

This Chesapeake, Virginia Wartsila Defense entity is the U.S. engineering/manufacturing operation for naval shaft-line and propulsion products, including propellers and shaft, rudder, and stern-tube seals. Public sources support those product families, but do not identify the exact DDG-51 new-construction item in this subaward.

Parent Vendor Name: JOHNSON CONTROLS INTERNATIONAL PUBLIC LIMITED COMPANY
Role / Description: The Marinette, Wisconsin Tyco Fire Products entity manufactures fire-suppression equipment and agents, including valves, nozzles, sprinklers, and foam-system products. Its exact DDG-51 shipset item is not publicly identified, so the role can only be confirmed at the fire-protection-system component level.
Supplies sediment-strainer elements and automatic backflushing strainers for DDG-51 fluid systems; NSN 4730-01-475-2135 identifies the Arleigh Burke class as an end product.

Distributes marine valves, pipe/tube, fittings, strainers/filtration, and bulkhead-sealing products for DDG construction. Public item data links W&O-supplied demineralizer components to the Arleigh Burke class, but the exact material mix in this subaward is not disclosed.

Supplies forged Navy anchor chain and detachable connecting links/deck chain fittings for DDG-51 anchoring and mooring systems. The current DDG subcontract is documented; public sources do not disclose the chain size or grade.

Performs ship preservation—abrasive blasting, surface preparation, and application/removal of protective coatings—plus structural repair support for Navy vessels. Public sources do not identify the specific DDG-51 compartment or coating work package in this subaward.

Supplies nonpowered shipboard valves—such as globe/stop/check and relief-valve assemblies and replacement parts—for DDG-51 fluid systems. Public NSN data identifies multiple Dante valve models with the Arleigh Burke class as the end item.

Supplies automatic bus-transfer-switch assemblies and internal electrical parts, including power transformers and switch components, used in DDG-51 shipboard distribution. Public NSN records identify ASCO ABT components for the class.

NAICS-6 Description: Air-Conditioning and Warm Air Heating Equipment and Commercial and Industrial Refrigeration Equipment Manufacturing
Role / Description: IMECO’s Marinette operation engineers and integrates marine HVAC, electrical, and machinery packages. A Navy notice linked to this UEI identifies a motor-and-brake assembly, but public sources do not disclose which DDG-51 shipboard system the subaward supported.

Despite the workbook label, this UEI resolves to Ellwood City Forge Company, a separate Ellwood plant that makes large open-die forgings, shafts, rotors, and other heavy steel components. I found no public source identifying which DDG-51 forging this site supplied.

Role / Description: This UEI is L3Harris Henschel’s Norfolk ship-control business. All Other Miscellaneous General Purpose Machinery Manufacturing

Role / Description: This is a legacy Tyco Fire Products Limited Partnership registration associated with fire-protection equipment. The exact entity-level DDG-51 component—and even the continuing operating site behind this UEI—could not be confirmed from public sources, so it should not inherit the Marinette sibling’s detailed product description.

NAICS-6 Description: Power Boiler and Heat Exchanger Manufacturing

Role / Description: This UEI is Wartsila/Defense Maritime Solutions’ Poulsbo, Washington registration, separate from the Chesapeake manufacturing entity. Its public footprint is West Coast installation, repair, overhaul, and parts support for naval shaft-line, propeller, and seal systems; an exact DDG-51 new-construction component was not publicly confirmed.

Subawardee Vendor Name: CONTINENTAL MICROWAVE AND TOOL CO., INC.

NAICS-6 Description: Air-Conditioning and Warm Air Heating Equipment and Commercial and Industrial Refrigeration Equipment Manufacturing
Role / Description: Supplies DDG-51 shipboard chilled-water air-conditioning plants, including 50-ton HFC-134a High-Efficiency Super-Capacity chillers. The Navy procures these plants to meet added cooling demand from DDG-51 electronic-warfare upgrades.

NAICS-6 Description: Ornamental and Architectural Metal Work Manufacturing

Role / Description: This UEI is Northrop Grumman Marine Systems in Sunnyvale, California. Northrop’s public filings identify the business as a producer of Virginia-class main-propulsion and ship-service turbine-generator equipment; the exact subaward line item is not publicly separated further.

Produces Virginia-class Propulsor Forward Assemblies (pump-jet propulsor hardware) and provides related design-engineering, sustainment hardware, and spares. Public awards cover manufacture of fixed propulsor assemblies for multiple boats.

D.C. Fabricators designs and manufactures steam condensers, heat exchangers, and corrosion-resistant alloy/dissimilar-metal fabrications for U.S. Navy submarine programs, including work associated with General Dynamics Electric Boat. Its public materials describe submarine and aircraft-carrier applications and emphasize military steam-condensing/heat-transfer hardware.

Globe Composite Solutions designs and manufactures composite submarine components. Its defense materials identify Los Angeles, Ohio, Seawolf, Virginia and Columbia submarines as key Navy platforms and describe work such as U.S. Navy gaskets, composite submarine parts for acoustic-signature reduction, and acoustic-intercept domes or related composites used in undersea applications.

NAICS-6 Description: Measuring, Dispensing, and Other Pumping Equipment Manufacturing
Role / Description: Curtiss-Wright Electro-Mechanical Systems is tied to Curtiss-Wright contracts for Virginia-class submarine equipment. Public Curtiss-Wright announcements identify EMS facilities as producing pumps, valves, generators and propulsors for Block IV Virginia-class submarines, with emphasis on nuclear propulsion systems and mission-critical naval machinery.

Scot Forge produces custom open-die and rolled-ring forgings for shipbuilding and defense programs, explicitly including the Virginia-class, Columbia-class, Seawolf-class and Los Angeles-class submarine programs. Its shipbuilding products include near-net-shape forgings such as hubs, gear blanks, seamless rolled rings, couplings, flanges, pinion shafts, step-downs, spindles, forged bars, gears, shafts, hollow tubes, blind-end cylinders and large flange cylinders.

Performs heavy fabrication and welding of large-scale, complex components for Virginia-class submarines. Public sources confirm class support but do not identify the specific installed component associated with this row.

Newport News Shipbuilding is the Virginia-class co-builder with General Dynamics Electric Boat. It fabricates and outfits large submarine modules, joins pressure-hull sections, and performs assembly, test, and delivery work on boats assigned to Newport News.

Portland Valve, a CIRCOR brand, manufactures high-performance Navy valves and controls, including ball valves and specialty valves for submarine applications. Sources describe Portland Valve shipments/orders for Block III Virginia-class submarine valves being built by General Dynamics Electric Boat and state that Portland Valve is a standard name in valves for submarines and surface vessels. Products include ship-service ball valves, butterfly valves, specialty submarine valves, controls and refurbished valves.

Teledyne D.G. O'Brien supplies high-reliability electrical interconnect hardware for Virginia-class submarines. Its submarine products include pressure-hull penetrators, connectors, and outboard cable assemblies; public procurement data confirms class-specific parts/material support but does not disclose the exact subaward part numbers.

NAICS-6 Description: Rubber Product Manufacturing for Mechanical Use
Role / Description: Supplies resilient weapon-system mounts installed on Virginia-class submarines. Federal logistics cross-reference data links Oil States part numbers 5MC10000-H and ND8714 to Electric Boat’s SSN-774 mount assembly.

NAICS-6 Description: Fluid Power Valve and Hose Fitting Manufacturing
Role / Description: VACCO Industries supplies critical-quiet fluid-control valves, manifolds, and filtration assemblies for Virginia-class submarines. VACCO reported nearly 40 of its valves/manifolds aboard USS North Dakota (SSN-784), distinguishing this entity as a quiet-valve and manifold supplier.

NAICS-6 Description: Air-Conditioning and Warm Air Heating Equipment and Commercial and Industrial Refrigeration Equipment Manufacturing
Parent Vendor Name: JOHNSON CONTROLS INTERNATIONAL PUBLIC LIMITED COMPANY
Role / Description: Johnson Controls Navy Systems is the York, Pennsylvania naval HVAC/refrigeration entity. It designs and manufactures submarine-qualified chillers, compressors, controls, and other environmental-control equipment; public sources do not identify the exact Virginia-class unit in this subaward.

Curtiss-Wright’s Cheswick, Pennsylvania electro-mechanical operation manufactures nuclear-fleet pumps, motors, generators, and related propulsion-plant machinery. The exact Virginia-class machine represented by this subaward is not publicly itemized, so the role cannot be narrowed beyond that equipment family.

Curtiss-Wright Flow Control is documented as a Virginia-class valve supplier, especially for nuclear-propulsion/reactor-plant applications. Public releases describe awards to provide critical valves for Virginia-class submarines, including reactor-plant valves for multiple shipsets, and identify Flow Control as designing and manufacturing highly engineered valves, pumps, motors, generators, electronics and systems for military/naval programs.

Austal USA is a submarine industrial-base capacity partner for General Dynamics Electric Boat. Public sources describe Austal USA fabricating and outfitting critical module components for Virginia-class and Columbia-class submarines, including orders for electronic deck modules and a command-deck module/structural assembly in support of the Virginia-class submarine program.

NAICS-6 Description: Laminated Plastics Plate, Sheet (except Packaging), and Shape Manufacturing
Role / Description: Goodrich is documented as a supplier of major composite structures for Virginia-class submarines. Public sources report Goodrich delivery of the first composite sail cusp for Virginia-class Block III submarines, contracts for lightweight composite sail cusps, composite components including a hydrodynamically shaped bow dome that houses/protects the sonar array, and later manufacturing support for Virginia-class propulsors.

The exact Virginia-class deliverable could not be confirmed publicly. APCO's naval business designs, manufactures, integrates, and installs advanced assembly and welding fixtures used to fabricate naval structures and components.

NAICS-6 Description: Capacitor, Resistor, Coil, Transformer, and Other Inductor Manufacturing
Role / Description: Produces the Circuit-D naval computer-controlled power-supply system, originally designed for the Virginia-class program and described as a critical platform electronic system. EMS also supplies parts and spares for advanced degaussing/signature-management systems.
Supplies linear electrical accelerometers for Virginia-class submarines. Federal logistics data identifies PCB part 631M15/003JV (NSN 6680-01-589-2611) as an SSN-774 end-item component.

Subawardee Vendor Name: KME AMERICA MARINE TUBE & FITTING, LLC
NAICS-6 Description: Other Metal Valve and Pipe Fitting Manufacturing
Parent Vendor Name: KME AMERICA MARINE TUBE & FITTING, LLC
Role / Description: KME America Marine Tube & Fitting manufactures complex metallic formed fittings and flanges and specializes in copper-nickel and high-performance-alloy fittings, flanges and piping components for demanding maritime/shipbuilding environments. Public sources describe it as a U.S. Navy subcontractor crafting piping and fittings for naval ships and submarines and as producing components for aircraft carriers and submarines.

Hunt Valve, a Fairbanks Morse Defense operation, is documented as a major supplier of valves for construction of new Virginia-class and Columbia-class submarines. Fairbanks Morse Defense announced purchase orders totaling $96 million to provide valves for 12 Virginia-class and five Columbia-class submarines, including ship-service ball valves and forged high-pressure system valves. Hunt/FMD also markets Navy-standard gate, globe, ball and relief valves.

Performs precision machining, welding, fabrication, and assembly for Electric Boat submarine work; public vendor records identify products such as pump housings, manifolds, and submarine torque tooling. No public source ties a specific part number to this Virginia-class row.

NAICS-6 Description: Fluid Power Cylinder and Actuator Manufacturing
Role / Description: This UEI is Moog’s Orrville, Ohio AMPAC-ISP operation, whose distinct product line is fluid-power cylinders and actuators. I found no public source naming the exact Virginia-class actuator supplied by this site, so the program role can be confirmed only at the fluid-power-actuation level.

W International SC was a South Carolina complex metal fabricator whose Goose Creek operation produced modules used in Virginia-class and Columbia-class submarine construction. HII/NNS acquired substantially all assets and workforce in 2025 to expand production of nuclear-powered submarine modules and aircraft-carrier units. Public reporting says modules from the Goose Creek operation include Virginia-class habitability, auxiliary machine room, and weapons modules.

NAICS-6 Description: Fabricated Pipe and Pipe Fitting Manufacturing
Role / Description: Supplies highly engineered naval-nuclear submarine fluid-system hardware, including proprietary valves, manifolds, pipe fittings, and flanges. Public filings identify these product lines as components for U.S. naval nuclear submarines.

NAICS-6 Description: Military Armored Vehicle, Tank, and Tank Component Manufacturing
Role / Description: W International, as a group/facility network, is publicly described as a manufacturer of large complex metal fabrications, shipbuilding structures, modules and assemblies. Public reporting states that W International produced modules for General Dynamics Electric Boat and Newport News and that the acquired Goose Creek facility made modules used in Virginia-class and Columbia-class submarine construction. Because this row is W International, LLC rather than the W International SC entity, I treat the role as the same module/fabrication family but with slightly lower UEI-specific certainty.

NAICS-6 Description: Measuring, Dispensing, and Other Pumping Equipment Manufacturing
Role / Description: This UEI is CIRCOR Naval Solutions/Warren Pumps in Warren, Massachusetts. It supplies engineered centrifugal pumps and pump components for submarine cooling, seawater, auxiliary, and other fluid systems; the exact Virginia-class pump service in this subaward is not publicly identified.

Supplies marine accommodation/joiner systems and interior hardware for Virginia-class submarines. A hardware supplier states that Jamestown commissioned the class's stainless-steel 3811-series mortise door-lock system.

Supplies heavy fabricated material for the Virginia-class propulsor forward assembly. A disclosed AT&F subcontract sits under BAE’s Navy contract for Virginia-class propulsor forward-subassembly material, manufacturing, and delivery.

Machines long, tight-tolerance submarine parts for Electric Boat using a dedicated facility built to bore and hone long components. Public sources confirm submarine manufacturing support but do not identify the specific Virginia-class part.

Designs and manufactures precision motors and generators for naval and submarine electrical machinery. The exact Virginia-class motor or generator model covered by the subaward was not publicly disclosed.

NAICS-6 Description: Administrative Management and General Management Consulting Services
Role / Description: Provides shipbuilding and program-management advisory services, including design-for-affordability, production planning, coordination, and industry expertise. The exact Virginia-class task performed under the subaward was not publicly described.

Through Granite State Manufacturing, produces precision-machined, welded, and electromechanical submarine components. Public reporting on Block V work says its products help submarines breathe, listen, and communicate, and identifies cable-handling systems; the exact product names were not released.

RIX Industries supplies high-pressure air compressor equipment for Virginia-class submarines. Public RIX and defense-contract reporting describe a multimillion-dollar U.S. Navy contract to deliver 25 Virginia-class high-pressure air compressors, continuing a long naval relationship. These compressors support submarine high-pressure air systems used for critical onboard functions.

NAICS-6 Description: Research and Development in the Physical, Engineering, and Life Sciences (except Nanotechnology and Biotechnology)
Role / Description: Develops, integrates, produces, and supports AN/BYG-1 submarine combat-control capabilities, including weapon-launch hardware and software, trainers/simulators, and systems engineering. AN/BYG-1 is fielded on Virginia-class submarines.

Supplies electrical-resistance temperature “lower probe” assemblies used in Virginia-class submarine engineering and diesel systems. NAICS-6 Description: Radio and Television Broadcasting and Wireless Communications Equipment Manufacturing
Role / Description: This UEI is an L3 Technologies corporate/Camden registration associated with communications and electronic systems contracting. Fabricates large, complex, and fracture-critical steel structures for Virginia- and Columbia-class submarines. Its work includes plate cutting and forming, precision machining, welding, and assembly.

Designs and manufactures critical electronic and electromechanical equipment for Virginia-class Blocks I-V. Public parts data links Aish terminal-board hardware to a Virginia-class data-acquisition unit, although the exact subaward configuration is not disclosed.

NAICS-6 Description: Ornamental and Architectural Metal Work Manufacturing
Role / Description: The exact Virginia-class component could not be confirmed publicly. Deltak is a custom metal fabricator providing laser/plasma cutting, forming, machining, robotic welding, and contract manufacturing.

NAICS-6 Description: Fabricated Pipe and Pipe Fitting Manufacturing
Role / Description: Supplies Level I Navy seamless and welded fittings, forgings, and pipe. Public transaction materials identify Custom Alloy as a dual source for Virginia-class main-seawater-system fittings.

Public parts-catalog data links Waukesha bearing hardware and associated temperature-sensing/probe components to Virginia-class submarine systems. The exact bearing assembly covered by this subaward was not publicly disclosed.

The exact Virginia-class deliverable could not be confirmed publicly. Rhoads performs heavy and custom metal fabrication, machining, welding, and shipboard/maritime construction and repair for Navy customers.

Supplied advanced low-maintenance valve-regulated lead-acid main storage battery systems for Virginia-class submarines. Public descriptions state that these batteries provide emergency/backup power and reactor-restart capability, including cells and associated packaging/trays.

Role / Description: Manufactures critical fittings and assembled components for Virginia- and Columbia-class submarines. It also performs final machining and assembly of additively manufactured submarine parts.

Supplies precision, cleanable all-metal wire-mesh filters and strainers for naval-nuclear submarine fluid systems. Public reporting connects the company's precision filters to Virginia-class construction, but does not identify the exact filter covered by this subaward.

Supplies Virginia-class shaft/bulkhead seal equipment and technical oversight for installation and maintenance. Wärtsilä's U.S. defense business specializes in marine shaft sealing and submarine propeller repair.

Produces cast metal components for submarine propulsion, fluid-control, weapons, and structural-support systems. Public examples include shaft sleeves, valve components, propeller hubs, and HY-80 steel castings; the exact Virginia-class casting is not public.

Provides U.S. Navy interior doors and closures fabricated in steel, aluminum, and stainless steel, plus related marine sheet-metal and repair work. Public sources did not identify the exact Virginia-class door or service covered by this subaward.

Manufactures welded and machined Navy hardware such as specialty fittings, flanges, union nuts, tees, caps, tailpieces, valves, and other machine components. The exact Virginia-class item was not publicly disclosed.

Provides SUBSAFE-authorized submarine modernization and repair plus precision fabrication, including installation, troubleshooting, maintenance, and repair of hull, mechanical and electrical, weapons, and main/auxiliary equipment. Role / Description: Fabricates, machines, and integrates large, complex metal parts and structures for submarine programs. Public sources do not identify the exact Virginia-class component covered by this subaward.

Manufactured pipe fittings, including elbow joints, for Virginia-class steam-piping systems. Public records identify NuFlo fittings as installed in the class, although they do not map this subaward to individual part numbers.

Role / Description: This UEI maps to CIRCOR Instrumentation Technologies’ Hoke/Gyrolok operation in Spartanburg, South Carolina. It supplies instrumentation valves, needle valves, manifolds, and tube fittings; public procurement evidence supports shipboard valve hardware, but does not identify the exact Virginia-class installation.

Fluid Power Valve and Hose Fitting Manufacturing

Role / Description: This UEI is Northrop Grumman Marine Systems in Sunnyvale. Northrop publicly assigns Sunnyvale work on the Columbia/Dreadnought common-missile-compartment launcher subsystem and also identifies Marine Systems as a supplier of Columbia-class power-generation equipment; the specific subaward line cannot be separated between those product lines from public data.

NAICS-6 Description: Power, Distribution, and Specialty Transformer Manufacturing
Role / Description: This UEI is Leonardo DRS’s Menomonee Falls naval-power plant, one of four sites named for Columbia electric-propulsion production. The facility manufactures solid-state propulsion drives and naval power-distribution hardware; DRS does not publicly itemize the exact drive, switchgear, or control unit by UEI.

Scot Forge is a custom forging supplier for marine propulsion and shipbuilding. Its public marine-propulsion materials describe experience producing ship, submarine, and boat shaft forgings, as well as open-die and rolled-ring forgings used as high-strength metal inputs for propulsion and other heavy mechanical applications. Defense-industrial-base coverage also identifies Scot Forge as part of the castings-and-forgings supplier base receiving submarine-industrial-base investment to expand large-forging capacity.

Manufactured Common Missile Compartment missile-tube assemblies at Rosyth for the U.S. Columbia and U.K. Dreadnought programs. Babcock later transferred these Rosyth operating activities to Rosyth Royal Dockyard Limited.

NAICS-6 Description: Measuring, Dispensing, and Other Pumping Equipment Manufacturing
Role / Description: Supplies electrical generators for Columbia-class submarines. Curtiss-Wright’s Electro-Mechanical Division received Columbia-specific generator-production contracts valued at approximately $120 million.

Rhoads performs large-scale defense maritime fabrication, assembly, outfitting, welding, pipefitting, and industrial services for Navy submarine construction. Its public materials describe a Philadelphia focus factory for Navy submarine construction, NAVSEA/MIL-spec welding qualifications, and General Dynamics Electric Boat approvals. Rhoads specifically describes moving completed submarine decks and modules from its manufacturing floor to barges bound for Navy test sites or final-assembly shipyard locations.

Precision Custom Components is a custom heavy-fabrication and nuclear-manufacturing supplier. Public company and acquisition materials describe custom-fabricated heavy pressure vessels, reactors, casks, and other heavy-walled components requiring specialized machining, welding, fabrication, engineering, drafting, and in-house manufacturing capability. Public subcontract data show a General Dynamics Electric Boat subaward under the Columbia-class design-completion contract, and local reporting describes PCC as supplying submarine components to the US Navy.
Manufactures missile-tube assemblies and Integrated Tube Hulls for the Common Missile Compartment shared by the Columbia and Dreadnought classes.

Graham supplies mission-critical defense turbomachinery, cryogenic pump, fluid-transfer, power, heat-transfer, and vacuum technology products. For Columbia-class submarines, Graham publicly announced a contract to provide the MK19 Air Turbine Pump assembly. The company also announced shipment of final first-article units for US Navy programs including Columbia, supporting the view that its work is a qualified equipment/subassembly item rather than raw material.

NAICS-6 Description: Military Armored Vehicle, Tank, and Tank Component Manufacturing
Role / Description: W International is an advanced metal-fabrication and distributed-shipbuilding supplier for nuclear-submarine structures. Public coverage of HII's acquisition of W International assets describes the Goose Creek, South Carolina facility as producing modules used in Virginia- and Columbia-class submarine construction. HII describes the site as supporting nuclear-powered submarine module and structure construction and producing completed submarine modules and structural units.

NAICS-6 Description: Air-Conditioning and Warm Air Heating Equipment and Commercial and Industrial Refrigeration Equipment Manufacturing
Parent Vendor Name: JOHNSON CONTROLS INTERNATIONAL PUBLIC LIMITED COMPANY
Role / Description: Johnson Controls Navy Systems supplies heating, ventilation, air-conditioning, and refrigeration systems for US Navy vessels, including submarines. Its public Navy/marine materials state that the company designs, manufactures, tests, installs, and supports naval HVAC and refrigeration equipment for platforms ranging from submarines to aircraft carriers. For submarine construction, its role is best characterized as environmental-control/HVAC-R machinery, components, and related engineering/support.

NAICS-6 Description: Fluid Power Valve and Hose Fitting Manufacturing
Role / Description: Marotta Controls supplies shipboard flow-control and motion-control hardware used on US Navy submarines and surface ships. Public product materials state that every US submarine goes to sea with Marotta ship controls and valves in mission-critical applications, and the company lists high-performance shipboard controls for propulsion, weapons, maneuverability, pressurization, fluid/gas collection and distribution, and other submarine systems. Relevant products include in-line valves, solenoid and motor-operated controls, and manifolds that integrate valves and fluid/gas pathways.

CP Industries manufactures high-pressure seamless steel flasks, cylinders, and pressure vessels for military, naval, and aerospace compressed-gas storage. Public materials describe CP as a key supplier of specialized high-pressure vessels to the US Navy for breathing air and shipboard uses, with products integrated into submarine systems for compressed gas storage. Recent Navy procurement data also identify CP Industries as a supplier of air flasks.

Fabricated large steel structures and modules for Columbia-class submarine construction at its Goose Creek, South Carolina facility. After HII acquired the operation, the same facility and legacy workforce were identified as producing Columbia auxiliary machinery-room and weapons modules.
Supplies high-integrity steel and stainless-steel castings for the Columbia program. Goodwin confirms U.S. Navy/Columbia work but does not disclose the exact casting or subsystem.

Hunt Valve supplies essential valves and electromechanical actuators for US Navy nuclear submarines. Fairbanks Morse Defense announced purchase orders for Hunt Valve to deliver valves for Virginia- and Columbia-class submarine programs, including Columbia hulls 4 through 7. The public announcement identifies ship service ball valves and forged high-pressure system valves, making Hunt a fluid/pressure-system valve supplier.

NAICS-6 Description: Turbine and Turbine Generator Set Units Manufacturing
Role / Description: This UEI is DRS Power Technology in Fitchburg, Massachusetts, a Columbia production site specializing in large electric machines and permanent-magnet propulsion technology. It is the DRS site most directly associated with the Columbia-class permanent-magnet main-propulsion motor, although the public contract announcement does not allocate every motor element by UEI.

Pegasus Steel is a nuclear-shipbuilding steel-fabrication supplier for complex structures and assemblies. Public acquisition and KEEL materials identify Pegasus/KEEL work on Columbia, Virginia, and Ford programs, including Columbia-class submarine superstructures and critical complex structures. Its history also references NAVSEA submarine work involving fracture-critical structures and components.

APCO publicly supplies submarine assembly and welding fixtures plus specialized handling equipment; shipment records to Electric Boat list J/K fixtures, welding trolleys, hydraulic jacks, caisson-lifting equipment, and related controls. The records reviewed do not explicitly identify which items correspond to its Columbia-class subawards.

The exact cargo billed to this Columbia subaward could not be confirmed. The best-supported role is defense freight forwarding, customs brokerage, and project-cargo logistics for UK-origin Common Missile Compartment hardware and its transportation/storage fixtures moving from Babcock Marine Rosyth to Electric Boat; public manifests identify C. H. Powell/Tandem as the notify/logistics party on such shipments.

NAICS-6 Description: Industrial and Commercial Fan and Blower and Air Purification Equipment Manufacturing
Role / Description: Cepeda Associates manufactures, services, and upgrades US Navy submarine life-support and atmosphere-control equipment, including Level I/SUBSAFE-qualified equipment. Its public materials describe submarine systems that remove carbon dioxide, carbon monoxide, hydrogen, volatile organic compounds, and related contaminants from the submarine atmosphere. Public Navy-contract notices also identify Cepeda as the original equipment manufacturer for Virginia-class submarine scrubbers and as a repair provider for those scrubbers.

NAICS-6 Description: Commercial and Industrial Machinery and Equipment (except Automotive and Electronic) Repair and Maintenance
Role / Description: This appears to be Columbia production infrastructure rather than installed shipboard hardware. Customs records associate Euro Machinery Specialists with delivery/service of large Innse-Berardi horizontal boring and milling centers for Electric Boat; those machines are used at Groton/Quonset to machine large submarine structures and mechanical components, although the specific Columbia part produced on them is not public.

Advance Mfg. provides precision machining, welded fabrication, large fabrication, and assemblies for defense, marine, power-generation, aerospace, and other high-reliability markets. Public materials list NAVSEA welding specifications, HY-80/HY-100 and related naval materials, large DoD fabrications, and customer certifications that include Electric Boat and Newport News Shipbuilding. Its history notes machined components used on military submarine periscopes, supporting a role as a precision-machined/fabricated component supplier rather than a module prime.

NAICS-6 Description: Fluid Power Valve and Hose Fitting Manufacturing
Role / Description: VACCO Industries supplies Columbia-class critical-quiet valves, manifolds, and fluid-control assemblies designed to minimize acoustic signature. VACCO’s own Navy literature lists both Virginia and Columbia among the submarine programs using these products.
Supplies complex hydraulic valves and manifolds for Columbia, including the rudder servovalve/manifold.

NAICS-6 Description: Capacitor, Resistor, Coil, Transformer, and Other Inductor Manufacturing
Role / Description: The exact Columbia unit is not disclosed. The best-supported inference is power-electronic equipment for the submarine’s advanced degaussing/magnetic-signature-control system—converters, current-regulation electronics, or spares that energize hull degaussing coils to cancel the boat’s magnetic field and reduce mine-detection vulnerability.

Austal USA fabricates and outfits critical module components for the US submarine industrial base. Austal describes a strategic partnership with General Dynamics Electric Boat for fabrication and outfitting of critical components for Virginia- and Columbia-class submarines. Public announcements identify Austal USA's first Columbia-class order as the Command and Control Systems Module for SSBN 828, and also describe work on CCSM and Electronic Deck Module content for submarine programs.

NAICS-6 Description: Rubber Product Manufacturing for Mechanical Use
Role / Description: A specific Columbia mount or filter is not publicly named. Oil States’ naval product line and earlier submarine supplier records point to elastomeric acoustic filters, shock mounts, and laminated bearings used under machinery or at piping/duct interfaces in machinery spaces to isolate pumps, motors, compressors, and other equipment from the hull and suppress structure-borne noise.

American Steel and Aluminum is a metal distributor, processor, and contract manufacturer serving defense and shipbuilding customers, including the submarine industrial base. Its public materials describe steel and aluminum sheet, plate, tube, pipe, bar, extrusions, structural shapes, cutting, forming, grinding, deburring, bending, machining, kitting, welding, and supply-chain control. Maine business reporting specifically says its Auburn, Massachusetts operation services General Dynamics Electric Boat's submarine industrial base and that the company cuts and forms plates and other parts for submarines.

NAICS-6 Description: Fluid Power Cylinder and Actuator Manufacturing
Role / Description: Supplies electrohydraulic linear actuators used on Columbia to operate shipboard valves, throttles, weapons-system mechanisms, doors, and hatches.

The class-specific valve model and fluid circuit are not public. Portland Valve’s confirmed submarine product is Navy-qualified ship-service ball valves and specialty valves, which would be distributed through Columbia’s seawater, cooling, hydraulic, and other auxiliary piping systems to isolate and control fluid flow.

NAICS-6 Description: Measuring, Dispensing, and Other Pumping Equipment Manufacturing
Role / Description: This UEI is CIRCOR Naval Solutions/Warren Pumps in Warren, Massachusetts. It supplies highly engineered centrifugal pumps and pump components for submarine fluid systems; the exact Columbia-class pump service represented by the subaward is not publicly disclosed.

Subawardee Vendor Name: L3HARRIS MARITIME POWER & ENERGY SOLUTIONS, INC.
Role / Description: Supplies switchgear and switchboard equipment for the Columbia-class shipboard electrical-distribution system; the program was described as the Ohio-class replacement when the award was announced.

Subawardee Vendor Name: GLOBE COMPOSITE SOLUTIONS, LTD., LIMITED PARTNERSHIP
Role / Description: Globe Composite Solutions designs and manufactures composite and elastomeric components for submarine defense and marine applications, with a focus on acoustic, hydrodynamic, sealing, and mission-critical parts. Public sources identify Globe as a critical supplier of more than 140 parts on Virginia- and Columbia-class submarines, including special hull-treatment-related parts, large-aperture bow acoustic panels, missile hatch gaskets, transducer covers, propulsor fairings, and propeller-shaft snubbers. Globe also describes long-running work designing composite submarine parts and acoustic-signature-reduction components.

Plainville Electrical Products Company, also branded PEPCO, designs and manufactures electrical switchboards and electrical distribution/control solutions for US Navy platforms, including advanced submarines. Its public materials state that it designs and manufactures electrical switchboards for advanced submarines, destroyers, and surface ships, and provides complete electrical distribution and control solutions. Federal-award profile data also match the company's switchgear and switchboard manufacturing business classification.

A Caterpillar subcontract is publicly reported under Electric Boat’s Columbia-class contract, but the disclosed record does not identify the purchased item. No Columbia-specific onboard component or construction service could be confirmed from public sources.

NAICS-6 Description: Fabricated Pipe and Pipe Fitting Manufacturing
Role / Description: Manufactures Level I high-alloy main-seawater pipe fittings for Columbia. Custom Alloy states it is the sole-source manufacturer approved for Columbia-class Main Seawater Fittings.

The exact Columbia heat exchanger is not publicly identified. DC Fabricators’ submarine specialty is large steam condensers, air ejectors, and other heat exchangers, making the strongest inference a main condenser/air-ejector or related secondary-steam-plant heat-transfer assembly in the engine room, where turbine exhaust is condensed and heat is rejected to seawater.

This UEI is Curtiss-Wright’s Target Rock operation in East Farmingdale, New York. It manufactures nuclear-qualified valves, actuators, and solenoid assemblies for submarine reactor and fluid systems; the exact Columbia-class valve or service is not publicly identified.

This is non-shipboard digital-engineering support. Beast Code’s Beast Core/IPX tools provide an interactive 2D/3D digital-twin environment that combines geometry, drawings, documents, and metadata for Columbia space arrangement, construction planning, work instructions, and lifecycle support; the exact licensed module or workflow is not disclosed.

NAICS-6 Description: All Other Miscellaneous Electrical Equipment and Component Manufacturing
Role / Description: Supplies power-conversion and magnetic components supporting Columbia's electric-drive propulsion system.
Supplies a sequence of large, high-integrity steel castings for the Columbia program, then called the Ohio replacement program. Public reporting does not disclose the casting geometry or subsystem.

Granite State Manufacturing has publicly said it received a new Columbia product but has not named it. The defensible description is a large, NAVSEA-qualified, precision-machined and welded mission-/life-critical submarine assembly—potentially a structural or equipment-support module—whose exact function and location remain nonpublic.

Public sources do not identify the exact Columbia assembly. Craft Machine Works’ relevant capability is final manufacture of large, tight-tolerance machined and complex-welded assemblies, so the subaward most likely covers a complete submarine machinery or structural/equipment-support assembly delivered for installation in a Columbia hull module; its precise location is unconfirmed.

A Columbia part number could not be confirmed. B & F Machine’s defense work includes five-axis machining of cases, diffusers, shrouds, rings, linkages, brackets, and mounts; the best-supported inference is precision flow-path or support hardware for a pump/turbomachinery or other mechanical system in the boat’s machinery/auxiliary spaces.
Fabricates bearing-support structures and related structural/propulsor demonstration hardware for Columbia's propulsor.
Manufactures Columbia hatches, fairings, hull inserts, and closure doors, and provides SUBSAFE structural fabrication and new-construction support.

Subawardee Vendor Name: KME AMERICA MARINE TUBE & FITTING, LLC
NAICS-6 Description: Other Metal Valve and Pipe Fitting Manufacturing
Parent Vendor Name: KME AMERICA MARINE TUBE & FITTING, LLC
Role / Description: The exact Columbia fitting schedule is not public. KME supplies complex formed elbows, tees, reducers, flanges, and tube fittings in copper-nickel, stainless, and other corrosion-resistant alloys; these parts are installed throughout Columbia’s seawater, cooling, and ship-service piping to route and join pressure-boundary fluid lines.

The one-time award pattern and company capability indicate Columbia production infrastructure, not shipboard hardware. Ingersoll most likely supplied a custom large-format milling/boring or automated manufacturing system used by Electric Boat to make large submarine structures, tooling, or mechanical components; neither the exact machine nor the Columbia part produced on it is public.

Curtiss-Wright’s Cheswick electro-mechanical operation produces nuclear-fleet pumps, motors, generators, and propulsion-plant machinery. Public sources do not disclose the exact Columbia-class machine represented by this subaward.

The exact Columbia forging is not publicly named. Lehigh Heavy Forge supplies super-heavy open-die shipbuilding forgings, including very long propulsion shafts and large rings; the subaward therefore most likely covers a high-integrity shaft, ring, or other major forging for the stern propulsion train or another pressure-/load-critical submarine assembly.
Supplies desurgers and bladder-type surge arrestors used on Columbia fluid systems.

The manufacturing entity most likely produced custom hydrodynamic thrust and/or journal-bearing assemblies for one of Columbia’s largest rotating machines—the electric propulsion motor, turbine-generators, or shaft line in the aft engineering spaces. Waukesha is a documented submarine main-thrust-bearing supplier, but the specific Columbia machine and bearing set remain undisclosed.

This is most likely shipyard production equipment rather than an onboard component. Koike supplies CNC plate-cutting systems, welding positioners, turning rolls, and manipulators used to rotate, align, cut, and weld very large hull/module fabrications; the exact Electric Boat workstation and Columbia assembly processed are not public.

The specific Columbia installation versus test use cannot be confirmed. PCB supplies low-noise, internally amplified accelerometers tied in Navy records to Electric Boat drawings; these sensors measure vibration and acoustic signatures on machinery and hull structures during quieting, production acceptance, and sea trials, and may also support onboard condition monitoring.
Manufactures composite bow domes for Columbia-class submarines.
Supplies Level I/SUBSAFE electrical hull penetrators that route transmitter cables through the Columbia pressure hull.

A specific Columbia casting could not be confirmed. The best-supported inference is high-integrity 70/30 copper-nickel or other alloy castings for seawater-system pump/valve bodies, with possible rotating-machinery bearing components; D. W. Clark is an approved Navy casting source and has prior SSBN bearing-casting work.

Confirmed service: Team Submarine selected Sopheon’s Accolade enterprise innovation and new-product-development platform. It supports strategic planning, technology roadmaps, project/portfolio governance, and resource planning across submarine programs including Columbia; it is shore-based program-management software, not equipment installed on the boat.

NAICS-6 Description: All Other Miscellaneous Fabricated Metal Product Manufacturing
Role / Description: The evidence points primarily to construction support rather than an installed boat component. Collins & Jewell builds large weldment fixtures, custom submarine support structures, and heavy handling/assembly equipment for Electric Boat’s Groton and Quonset facilities; the specific Columbia fixture or support structure is not publicly named.

NAICS-6 Description: Commercial and Service Industry Machinery Manufacturing
Role / Description: The UEI maps to Parker’s Village Marine Tec water-purification operation in Carson, California. The best-supported Columbia deliverable is a reverse-osmosis seawater desalination/watermaker package—high-pressure pumps, membranes, controls, and service parts—installed in an auxiliary machinery space to produce potable water; the exact model and capacity are not public.

Leslie Controls manufactures severe-service regulating, pressure-reducing, and control valves. Its Columbia-class contribution is valve and fluid-control hardware, but public sources do not identify the exact valve set or shipboard service.

The strongest class-specific linkage is to the Columbia propulsor forward subassembly. American Tank & Fabricating most likely forms and welds heavy plate components or supporting structures for that pump-jet assembly at the stern, but the public contract data do not expose the exact plate part, drawing, or subassembly element.

NAICS-6 Description: Research and Development in the Physical, Engineering, and Life Sciences (except Nanotechnology and Biotechnology)
Role / Description: These subawards sit under Office of Naval Research submarine science-and-technology contracts rather than production hardware buys. The best-supported role is advanced modeling, simulation, sensing/acoustics, networking, or autonomous-payload engineering intended to mature concepts for transition to Virginia and Columbia; the exact BBN work package and any eventual onboard system cannot be confirmed.

Constructs and expands Electric Boat production facilities used for Columbia manufacturing, including hull-fabrication/advanced-manufacturing buildings and missile-tube production infrastructure. This is shipyard infrastructure, not an onboard component.

No installed Columbia component could be substantiated. Greenland’s documented work is design-build construction of energy and utility infrastructure—HVAC, chilled-water/steam piping, controls, and building retrofits—so the subaward most likely funded a shipyard or mission-critical-facility utility project supporting Columbia production at Electric Boat or Newport News.
Provides long-haul truckload transportation and logistics for Columbia construction. The specific cargo carried is not publicly disclosed.

NAICS-6 Description: Measuring, Dispensing, and Other Pumping Equipment Manufacturing
Role / Description: This UEI is CIRCOR Pumps North America’s IMO Pump operation in Monroe, North Carolina. It supplies Navy rotary/screw pump-and-motor assemblies and related pump components; the exact Columbia-class fluid-system application is not publicly identified.

NAICS-6 Description: Fabricated Pipe and Pipe Fitting Manufacturing
Role / Description: Manufactures specialized alloy forgings, custom fittings, and pipe for naval and defense applications. As successor to Custom Alloy's High Bridge operation, it continues the fitting/forging business; public sources do not separately identify which post-acquisition Columbia fitting each subaward covers.

Subawardee Vendor Name: EMERSON AUTOMATION SOLUTIONS FINAL CONTROL US LP
Parent Vendor Name: EMERSON AUTOMATION SOLUTIONS FINAL CONTROL US LP
Role / Description: The exact Columbia valve tag and circuit are not publicly disclosed. Emerson’s Mansfield Navy product operation supplies pressure-/safety-relief, control, and angle valves; these protect submarine piping and equipment from overpressure and regulate flow in machinery and auxiliary systems.

Provides large-scale precision welding, fabrication, and machining of heavy metal components for Virginia- and Columbia-class submarine programs. Public sources do not disclose the exact Columbia part.

A particular Columbia filter model and fluid circuit are not public. Fluid Conditioning Products makes cleanable all-metal mesh/coalescing filter and strainer elements qualified for Navy SUBSAFE Level I service, making the likely deliverable pressure-boundary filtration for seawater, hydraulic, lubricating-oil, fuel, or another critical machinery-space fluid system.

Public sources confirm that Fairlead supplies shipboard integrated parts for Virginia- and Columbia-class submarines but do not name this assembly. Based on its nuclear-shipbuilding capabilities, the best-supported inference is a precision-fabricated/machined mechanical subassembly, machinery support/skid, or integrated power/control structure installed in a Columbia machinery or electrical module; the exact item remains unconfirmed.
Develops and administers workforce training and learning content for Electric Boat personnel building Virginia- and Columbia-class submarines.

The best public evidence identifies PRL as a Columbia supplier of critical cast pump and valve components. PRL pours, heat-treats, machines, and nondestructively tests high-integrity bodies and other cast parts used in the boat’s seawater, cooling, hydraulic, and other machinery/auxiliary fluid systems; the exact pump/valve model is not public.
